prerequisites: A Cisco Meraki Dashboard account with admin privileges in the target organization (https://dashboard.meraki.com).
API access generated under Organization > API & Webhooks > API keys and access in the Meraki dashboard, which produces the api_key value.
A start_date marking the earliest point from which to sync data.

authMethods.name: API key (Bearer token)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with the api_key secret field. The Meraki Dashboard API v1 accepts the key as a Bearer token via the standard HTTP Authorization header (Authorization: Bearer <api_key>). Generate the key from Organization > API & Webhooks > API keys and access; you must already be an admin in the organization. Treat the key like a password.

setupSteps.title: Sign in as an organization admin
setupSteps.body: Log in to the Meraki Dashboard at https://dashboard.meraki.com with an account that has admin privileges on the organization you want to sync.

setupSteps.title: Generate an API key
setupSteps.body: Go to Organization > API & Webhooks, open the API keys and access tab, and generate a new API key. Copy the key value immediately and store it securely, since it is shown only once.

setupSteps.title: Provide the api_key
setupSteps.body: Paste the generated key into the connector's required api_key field. The connector passes it to the Dashboard API v1 as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.

setupSteps.title: Set the start_date
setupSteps.body: Enter the required start_date to define the earliest date from which the connector should begin pulling data.
